prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a children's book about a shy octopus who wants to make friends.
response: Deep down at the bottom of the bluest part of the sea, where sunlight danced in wobbly ribbons and seaweed swayed like sleepy dancers, there lived a little octopus named Oliver. Oliver had eight curly arms, two big round eyes, and one very shy heart. Whenever another sea creature swam by, Oliver would turn the exact color of the rock behind him and tuck himself into the tiniest ball an octopus could possibly make. But secretly—so secretly that even the seahorses didn't know—Oliver wished more than anything in the whole wide ocean that he could find a friend.
